title: stellaria media var. apetala
intro: Stellaria media var. apetala, also known as lesser celandine, is a flowering plant that belongs to the Caryophyllaceae family, characterized by its bright yellow flowers and heart-shaped leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds. The medicinal properties of lesser celandine have been found to have various health benefits, including the treatment of skin conditions, such as eczema and acne, as well as the reduction of swelling and pain. Herbal preparations of lesser celandine can be made into teas, tinctures, and topical creams, allowing for its medicinal properties to be utilized in a variety of ways.
